description: Every member of a relevant set falls under 'theula': all people, every day, the whole amount. Context may supply the set when it is not named. 'theli' (EACH) distributes a claim member by member; 'theula' presents the total scope together.
articulatory_notes: Begin with voiceless dental 'th', the tongue slightly exposed and air continuous, then withdraw into front 'e' at half-open height with unrounded lips. Preserve the hiatus before rounded high-back 'u', which carries stress. For 'la', make lateral contact at the alveolar ridge and finish with open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: Because the opening is shared with 'theli' (EACH), the universal pair are easy to hear as kin. The hiatus gives 'theula' a broader span before 'la' opens outward, a contour that can suggest the set taken together.
usage_notes: A noun quantified by 'theula' carries no second quantity form. The quantifier may stand alone when the domain is understood, as in 'theula kelu', everything becomes. With 'thimu' (time), 'theula thimu' gives the canonical always.
